description: Defines the controlled compatibility relationship vocabulary for the Compatibility Ontology. These relationships connect compatibility entities to each other and to Layer 1 domain entities. Direction is canonical; virtual inverses are never materialized. This vocabulary is designed for compatibility with both Neo4j property graphs and Qdrant vector stores.
design_principles: All relationships are directional; the canonical direction is always from source to target.
Virtual inverse labels support query traversal only and must not be stored as duplicate edges.
Compatibility relationships (REQUIRES, SUPPORTS, CONFLICTS_WITH) always require explicit version conditions.
Lifecycle relationships track rule provenance and supersession chains.
Evidence and validation relationships preserve the auditability of every approved rule.
Derived and replacement relationships enable future-safe ontology evolution without breaking existing rule identities.

relationships.description: The source component or rule asserts that the target component is a mandatory prerequisite under the defined version scope and conditions. Violating this relationship produces a compliance failure of the declared severity.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-BIOS-001) REQUIRES VersionConstraint(VC-FW-FIRMWARE-001) — expresses that BIOS 6.4.2 requires Firmware >= 8.2.0.

relationships.description: The source component officially supports operation, integration, or use with the target component under explicitly defined conditions. Must be backed by an authoritative source statement. Direction reflects the supporting entity pointing to the supported entity.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-DRV-001) SUPPORTS VersionConstraint(VC-DRV-PDP-001) — expresses that System Firmware 8.2.1 supports Platform Driver Pack >= 12.5.0.

relationships.description: The source component configuration is documented to conflict or operate incorrectly when co-existing with the target component configuration under the specified conditions. Must be backed by authoritative evidence; suspected incompatibilities are insufficient.
relationships.usage_example: ConflictConstraint(CC-FW-BIOS-001) CONFLICTS_WITH VersionConstraint(VC-FW-OLD-001) — BIOS 6.4.2 conflicts with Firmware < 8.0.0.

relationships.description: The source issue, vulnerability, or known defect is resolved by upgrading to the target component version. FIXED_BY links a known issue record or security advisory to the specific VersionConstraint that eliminates the issue.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-SEC-BIOS-001) FIXED_BY VersionConstraint(VC-BIOS-642-001) — Privilege escalation vulnerability SEC-2026-001 is fixed by BIOS >= 6.4.2.

relationships.description: The source component at its current version state must be upgraded to the target version state as the prescribed remediation action. UPGRADE_TO formalizes the directed upgrade path from a non-compliant state to a compliant state.
relationships.usage_example: Remediation(REM-DRV-001) UPGRADE_TO VersionConstraint(VC-DRV-125-001) — remediation path upgrades Driver Pack to >= 12.5.0.

relationships.description: The source component has an operational dependency on the target component. Distinct from REQUIRES in that DEPENDS_ON may represent field-observed or implicit dependencies not explicitly stated in vendor documentation.
relationships.usage_example: DependencyConstraint(DC-SEC-MGT-001) DEPENDS_ON VersionConstraint(VC-MGT-370-001) — Security Agent 4.8.3 operationally depends on Endpoint Management Agent >= 3.7.0.

relationships.description: The source Remediation or UpdateSequenceConstraint resolves or mitigates the target CompatibilityRule violation. REMEDIATES is the operational bridge between a detected non-compliance and the corrective prescription.
relationships.usage_example: Remediation(REM-FW-BIOS-001) REMEDIATES CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-BIOS-001) — the firmware upgrade remediates the BIOS prerequisite violation.

relationships.description: The source component is supported in operation or integration by the target authoritative declaration, policy, or rule. SUPPORTED_BY is the virtual inverse of SUPPORTS; it should not be materialized but is defined here for query vocabulary completeness.
relationships.notes: This is the virtual inverse of SUPPORTS. Do not materialize as a stored edge. Use SUPPORTS in canonical direction and traverse backward in queries.
relationships.usage_example: Query traversal only: VersionConstraint(VC-DRV-PDP-001) SUPPORTED_BY CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-DRV-001).

relationships.description: The source component configuration or rule blocks the target upgrade, installation, or migration from proceeding. BLOCKS is used when an UpdateSequenceConstraint or ConflictConstraint explicitly prevents a downstream operation from being safely executed.
relationships.usage_example: UpdateSequenceConstraint(USC-FW-BIOS-001) BLOCKS Remediation(REM-BIOS-642-001) — Firmware must be updated before BIOS upgrade can proceed.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule or VersionConstraint supersedes the target as a more recent, authoritative, or corrected assertion. The target rule is moved to the 'superseded' lifecycle state. SUPERSEDES maintains the full provenance chain.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-OS-002) SUPERSEDES CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-OS-001) — a revised rule replaces an earlier version of the same compatibility assertion.

relationships.description: The source entity (rule, exception, remediation) cites the target CompatibilityDocument or Evidence record as a supporting or contextual reference. REFERENCES is the lightweight evidence link used when a full VALIDATED_BY or APPROVED_BY chain is not yet established.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-DRV-OS-001) REFERENCES CompatibilityDocument(DOC-CA114A84AE60) — the rule is sourced from the identified advisory document.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule owns or applies the target Condition as an applicability gate. The presence of all or any conditions (per condition_logic) determines whether the rule fires for a given system state.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-DRV-OS-001) HAS_CONDITION Condition(COND-001) — the rule applies only when Enterprise OS 2026.1 is installed.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule has the target Exception as a scoped exclusion. The Exception narrows the rule's applicability by defining device families, platforms, or configurations for which the rule does not fire.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-DRV-NIC-001) HAS_EXCEPTION Exception(EXC-NICFW-001) — ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series are exempt from the NIC Firmware requirement.

relationships.description: The source entity (CompatibilityRule, Exception, or Remediation) is substantiated by the target Evidence record. HAS_EVIDENCE is the primary auditability link and must be established before a rule can progress beyond the candidate lifecycle state.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-DRV-OS-001) HAS_EVIDENCE Evidence(EVID-CA114A84AE60-001) — the rule is backed by an extracted excerpt from the advisory document.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule or ConflictConstraint prescribes the target Remediation as the corrective action that resolves a detected compliance failure. A rule may have multiple remediations representing alternative or sequenced fix paths.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-BIOS-001) HAS_REMEDIATION Remediation(REM-FW-BIOS-001) — the rule prescribes a firmware upgrade as its remediation.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule or VersionConstraint has been formally validated by the target validation report. VALIDATED_BY records the automated validation pass that is a prerequisite for the approved lifecycle state.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-BIOS-001) VALIDATED_BY OntologyValidationReport(VAL-RPT-001) — the rule passed the automated validation gate.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule has been reviewed and formally approved by the target human reviewer or approval authority. APPROVED_BY is the governance record that promotes a rule from validated to approved lifecycle state.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-BIOS-001) APPROVED_BY ApprovalRecord(APR-001) — the rule was approved by the designated reviewer.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule was derived from the target raw rule candidate (RCAND-*) or source document chunk. DERIVED_FROM preserves the provenance chain from the raw extraction layer to the governed Compatibility Ontology.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-DRV-OS-001) DERIVED_FROM Evidence(EVID-RCAND-000360-001) — the governed rule was derived from raw candidate RCAND-000360.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule replaces the target rule as its authoritative successor. The target rule must be moved to the superseded or archived lifecycle state. REPLACES preserves identity continuity in the rule graph.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-OS-002) REPLACES CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-OS-001) — version 2 of a rule replaces version 1 after a compliance boundary update.

relationships.description: The source CompatibilityRule or Remediation targets the target Layer 1 entity (by entity_id from canonical_entity_registry). TARGETS is the cross-layer join link between the Compatibility Ontology and the Domain Knowledge Layer, enabling compliance evaluation against known domain entities.
relationships.notes: target_types references canonical entities in the Layer 1 registry (DRV-*, FW-*, OS-*, SEC-*, MGT-* namespaces). This is the primary cross-layer relationship in the engine.
relationships.usage_example: CompatibilityRule(CRULE-FW-BIOS-001) TARGETS Layer1CanonicalEntity(FW-001) — the rule applies to the canonical BIOS entity in the Domain Knowledge Layer.

neo4j_compatibility_note: All relationship_name values are suitable for use as Neo4j relationship types. source_types and target_types define the allowed node label combinations for each relationship. TARGETS relationship enables join queries between CompatibilityLayer nodes and Domain Layer nodes.
qdrant_compatibility_note: REFERENCES, HAS_EVIDENCE, and DERIVED_FROM relationships connect to Evidence entities whose source_excerpt fields are embedding candidates. These relationships enable vector similarity searches to identify semantically related rules.
